title: Kharaj Mukherjee - Wikipedia
body: Bengali actor Kharaj Mukherjee Kharaj Mukherjee, in an interview, in 2018 Born ( 1963-07-07 ) 7 July 1963 (age 62) Rampurhat , Birbhum , West Bengal , India Other name Kharaj Mukhopadhyay Occupations Actor, Singer Known for Acting and Singing Children 1 Kharaj Mukherjee or Kharaj Mukhopadhyay is an Indian actor, comedian and singer. He won the Bengal Film Journalists' Association – Best Male Playback Award in 2004 for the film Patalghar . Over the years, he has established himself as one of the finest actors in the Bengali film industry due to his versatility in various roles. [ 1 ] [ 2 ] [ 3 ] [ 4 ] Career [ edit ] He completed his education from St. Lawrence High School, Kolkata [ 5 ] and completed his graduation in B.A from University of calcutta . Before starting his film career, he worked in the Indian Railways . [ 6 ] He made his debut in Bengali film with the film Hulusthul in 1980. In the last 32 years, he has worked in many films like Patalghar , Bye Bye Bangkok , Kahani , Namesake , [ 7 ] Accident , Muktodhara , Special 26 , Lafangey Parindey , [ 8 ] Yuva , [ 8 ] Parineeta , [ 8 ] Laga Chunari Mein Daag , [ 8 ] Chha-e Chhuti , Jaatishwar etc. He is known for his excellence in both Commercial and natural acting. In 2012 film Kahaani , Mukherjee played the role of Inspector Chatterjee. His son, Bihu Mukherjee debuted in the film Abar Basanta Bilap .
